cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this problem. The problem says that Jane quit her job on March 20, 2020. 176 days have passed since then, and we need to find out what the date will be tomorrow in the format MM/DD/YYYY. There are multiple options provided, so I need to verify each step carefully to avoid mistakes. First, I need to calculate the date 176 days after March 20, 2020. Then, since the question asks for the date tomorrow, I have to add one more day to that result. Let me start by calculating the date 176 days after March 20, 2020. Let's break it down step by step. March has 31 days, so from March 20 to March 31, there are 11 days (since 31 - 20 = 11). So adding those 11 days brings us to March 31, 2020. That accounts for 11 days out of the 176. Now, April 2020 has 30 days. Adding those 30 days brings the total to 11 + 30 = 41 days. So we're at April 30, 2020. That's 41 days. May 2020 has 31 days. Adding another 31 days brings the total to 41 + 31 = 72 days. So now it's May 31, 2020. June 2020 has 30 days. Adding that gives 72 + 30 = 102 days. So June 30, 2020. July 2020 has 31 days. Adding that gives 102 + 31 = 133 days. So July 31, 2020. August 2020 has 31 days. Adding 31 days to July 31 brings us to August 31, 2020. The total days so far would be 133 + 31 = 164 days. Now, 176 - 164 = 12 days remaining. So we need to add 12 days to August 31, 2020. That would take us into September. August 31 + 12 days = September 12, 2020. Wait, but is that correct? Let me check: If August has 31 days, then adding 1 day to August 31 is September 1. Adding 12 days would be September 1 + 11 days = September 12. So
